diff:
diff --git a/doc.go b/doc.go
index 8060135..4b0ed70 100644
--- a/doc.go
+++ b/doc.go
@@ -179,6 +179,13 @@ or "reversed". We define a name calling Name() on a route. For example:
 	r.HandleFunc("/articles/{category}/{id:[0-9]+}", ArticleHandler).
 	  Name("article")
 
+Route names must be unique across all routes registered on a router. Assigning
+the same name to two different routes is an error: the second call to Name()
+records an error on the route (retrievable via Route.GetError()) and the
+duplicate name is not written to the router's name registry. Use
+Router.Get(name) to retrieve a route by name; it returns nil if the name is
+not registered.
+
 To build a URL, get the route and call the URL() method, passing a sequence of
 key/value pairs for the route variables. For the previous route, we would do:
 
diff --git a/route.go b/route.go
index d10401e..44e818f 100644
--- a/route.go
+++ b/route.go
@@ -209,14 +209,21 @@ func (r *Route) GetHandlerWithMiddlewares() http.Handler {
 
 // Name sets the name for the route, used to build URLs.
 // It is an error to call Name more than once on a route.
+// It is also an error to assign a name that is already registered by
+// a different route; the second registration records the error on the route
+// and the duplicate name is not written to the namedRoutes map.
 func (r *Route) Name(name string) *Route {
 	if r.name != "" {
 		r.err = fmt.Errorf("mux: route already has name %q, can't set %q",
 			r.name, name)
 	}
 	if r.err == nil {
-		r.name = name
-		r.namedRoutes[name] = r
+		if existing, ok := r.namedRoutes[name]; ok && existing != r {
+			r.err = fmt.Errorf("mux: route with name %q already registered", name)
+		} else {
+			r.name = name
+			r.namedRoutes[name] = r
+		}
 	}
 	return r
 }

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing check `if r.name != ""` only prevents renaming the same route. A different route can still set the same name and silently overwrite namedRoutes. Adding `if existing, ok := r.namedRoutes[name]; ok && existing != r` catches the cross-route collision.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix is minimal: one extra if-block in Name(). The doc.go update is also straightforward.

alternatives: Return a separate error from Name() (changing signature)
key_decisions.decision: Store error on r.err like existing duplicate-name-on-same-route behavior
key_decisions.reasoning: Consistent with existing error-on-route pattern; does not change the Name() signature or break existing callers

solve_notes: In route.go's Name() method, added a check after the existing same-route name check: if namedRoutes[name] exists and it's a different route (existing != r), store an error on r.err and skip registration. Updated doc.go to document that route names must be unique and that a duplicate name records an error on the route.
task_text:
Fix Route.Name silently overwriting names registered by other routes

When two different routes both call `.Name("api")`, the second
registration silently overwrites the first in the `namedRoutes` map
in `route.go`. `Router.Get("api")` then returns the wrong route with
no warning. The existing check in `Name()` only prevents renaming the
same route. Fix `Name()` to return an error when the name is already
registered by a different route in the `namedRoutes` map. Update
`doc.go` to document the name uniqueness constraint and the new error
return behavior.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: tests that Name() records an error when called twice on the same route; directly exercises the Name() function's error path and covers the changed namedRoutes assignment block
tests that multiple routes with different names all register in namedRoutes; exercises the successful Name() path that was refactored into the else branch

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Route
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: namedRoutes field is a shared map reference (set from parent router) — understanding this prevents wrong assumption that namedRoutes is route-local

thrash_preventing_defs.name: NewRoute
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows that all routes created by a router share the same namedRoutes map pointer — confirms the uniqueness check is straightforward via r.namedRoutes[name]

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the two files directly edited. The thrash_preventing set adds Route struct (so an agent understands that namedRoutes is a shared map pointer, not per-route storage) and NewRoute (to confirm all routes on a router share the same namedRoutes map). Without these, an agent might search for how namedRoutes gets populated and whether checking r.namedRoutes[name] is correct before implementing the fix.
